summary_en: Adam was the first human being, the father of humanity, and the first prophet of Islam, created by Allah from clay.
summary_id: Adam adalah manusia pertama, bapak umat manusia, dan nabi pertama dalam Islam, diciptakan oleh Allah dari tanah liat.
story_en: Allah created Adam from clay and breathed life into him. He was taught the names of all things, showcasing human intellect. Allah commanded the angels and Iblis to prostrate before Adam. While the angels obeyed, Iblis refused out of pride. Adam and his wife Hawwa (Eve) lived in Paradise but were tricked by Iblis into eating from the forbidden tree. Sent to Earth, they repented sincerely, and Allah forgave them. Adam established the first worship of Allah on Earth and lived for about 1,000 years, guiding his children in monotheism.
story_id: Allah menciptakan Adam dari tanah liat dan meniupkan ruh kehidupan ke dalamnya. Dia diajarkan nama-nama segala sesuatu, menunjukkan kecerdasan manusia. Allah memerintahkan para malaikat dan Iblis untuk bersujud kepada Adam. Para malaikat patuh, namun Iblis menolak karena sombong. Adam dan istrinya Hawwa (Hawa) tinggal di Surga tetapi ditipu oleh Iblis hingga memakan buah dari pohon terlarang. Diturunkan ke Bumi, mereka bertaubat dengan tulus dan Allah mengampuni mereka. Adam memulai ibadah pertama kepada Allah di Bumi dan hidup sekitar 1.000 tahun, membimbing keturunannya dalam tauhid.
miracles_en: Created directly by Allah's hands from clay, receiving the divine breath of life, being taught the names of all things, and having the angels prostrate to him in respect.
miracles_id: Diciptakan langsung oleh tangan Allah dari tanah liat, menerima tiupan ruh kehidupan ilahi, diajarkan nama-nama segala sesuatu, dan para malaikat bersujud kepadanya sebagai bentuk penghormatan.

summary_en: Idris was a prophet celebrated for his wisdom, honesty, and pioneering contributions to writing, sewing, and astronomy.
summary_id: Idris adalah seorang nabi yang terkenal karena kebijaksanaan, kejujuran, dan kontribusi perintisnya dalam menulis, menjahit, dan astronomi.
story_en: Prophet Idris was born during a time when humanity began to forget the monotheistic teachings of Adam and Seth, and corruption started to spread. He called his people back to righteousness and worship of Allah alone. Idris was blessed with extraordinary intellect and was a pioneer of civilization; he was the first human to write with a pen, the first to sew and wear tailored clothes (instead of animal skins), and he possessed deep knowledge of astronomy, mathematics, and urban planning. He led campaigns against the corrupt descendants of Qabil (Cain) and remained steadfast in worship. Due to his truthfulness, patience, and devotion, Allah raised him alive to a high station in the fourth heaven, as confirmed in the Quran and during the Night Journey of Prophet Muhammad.
story_id: Nabi Idris lahir pada masa ketika umat manusia mulai melupakan ajaran tauhid dari Nabi Adam dan Syits, dan kerusakan mulai menyebar di bumi. Beliau menyeru kaumnya untuk kembali ke jalan yang lurus dan menyembah Allah semata. Idris dianugerahi kecerdasan yang luar biasa dan menjadi pelopor peradaban; beliau adalah manusia pertama yang menulis dengan pena, orang pertama yang menjahit dan mengenakan pakaian berpotongan (bukan kulit binatang), serta memiliki pengetahuan mendalam tentang astronomi, matematika, dan perencanaan kota. Beliau memimpin perjuangan melawan keturunan Qabil yang durhaka. Karena kejujuran, kesabaran, dan keteguhannya, Allah mengangkatnya hidup-hidup ke kedudukan yang tinggi di langit keempat.
miracles_en: Blessed with extraordinary wisdom, being the first to write and sew, and being raised by Allah to an exceptionally high station in the heavens.
miracles_id: Diberkahi dengan kebijaksanaan luar biasa, menjadi orang pertama yang menulis dan menjahit, serta diangkat oleh Allah ke kedudukan yang sangat tinggi di langit.

summary_en: Nuh was a resilient messenger who preached monotheism for 950 years and built the Great Ark by divine instruction to survive the global deluge.
summary_id: Nuh adalah rasul yang tangguh yang berdakwah tauhid selama 950 tahun dan membangun Bahtera Besar atas petunjuk ilahi untuk selamat dari air bah global.
story_en: Prophet Nuh was sent to a society that had fallen deeply into idol worship, worshipping five righteous men who had passed away (Wadd, Suwa', Yaghuth, Ya'uq, and Nasr). For 950 years, Nuh preached patiently, using every means—publicly and privately, night and day—to guide them back to monotheism. Only a small group of poor and weak citizens believed in him, while the wealthy chiefs mocked and threatened him. When their disbelief became absolute and they challenged him to bring the punishment, Allah commanded Nuh to build a massive Ark. Despite his people's intense ridicule for building a ship in a land far from any sea or river, he completed it. The Great Flood arrived; water gushed from the earth and poured from the sky. Nuh, the believers, and pairs of every animal species boarded the Ark and were saved, while the disbelievers—including Nuh's own wife and his son Yam (who refused to join and tried to seek refuge on a mountain)—perished in the deluge. The Ark eventually came to rest on Mount Judi, and Nuh helped rebuild humanity on the foundation of monotheism.
story_id: Nabi Nuh diutus kepada masyarakat yang telah tenggelam dalam penyembahan berhala (Wadd, Suwa', Yaghuts, Ya'uq, dan Nasr). Selama 950 tahun, Nuh berdakwah dengan sabar siang dan malam, baik secara sembunyi-sembunyi maupun terang-terangan. Namun, hanya sebagian kecil orang miskin dan lemah yang beriman, sementara para pembesar yang kaya mengejek dan mengancamnya. Ketika keingkaran mereka telah mencapai puncaknya, Allah memerintahkan Nuh membangun sebuah Bahtera besar. Meskipun diejek karena membuat kapal di daratan kering tanpa laut atau sungai, Nuh tetap menyelesaikannya. Banjir Besar pun datang; air memancar dari bumi dan tercurah dari langit. Nuh, orang-orang beriman, dan sepasang dari setiap spesies hewan diselamatkan di dalam Bahtera, sementara orang-orang kafir—termasuk istri Nuh dan putranya, Kan'an (Yam)—tenggelam. Bahtera akhirnya berlabuh di Bukit Judi.
miracles_en: Building the massive Ark under divine guidance in a dry land, and surviving the catastrophic global deluge that wiped out the entire disbelieving population.
miracles_id: Membangun Bahtera raksasa di bawah bimbingan ilahi di tanah gersang, dan selamat dari air bah global dahsyat yang menyapu bersih seluruh populasi orang kafir.

summary_en: Hud was sent to the mighty and arrogant giants of the 'Ad tribe, warning them against pride before they were destroyed by a fierce wind.
summary_id: Hud diutus kepada suku 'Ad yang bertubuh raksasa dan sombong, memperingatkan mereka sebelum akhirnya dihancurkan oleh angin kencang.
story_en: Prophet Hud was sent to the tribe of 'Ad, a powerful civilization residing in the sandy hills of Al-Ahqaf (between Yemen and Oman). The people of 'Ad were famous for their giant stature, immense physical strength, and magnificent palaces carved out of mountains. However, they became arrogant, oppressed the weak, and worshipped idols. Hud called them to worship Allah alone and show gratitude for their blessings. They rejected his call, calling him a liar and a fool. After a long three-year drought failed to soften their hearts, they saw a massive dark cloud approaching and rejoiced, thinking it would bring rain. Instead, it was Allah's punishment: a freezing, violent windstorm (Sarsar) that raged for seven nights and eight days, ripping the giants from their homes and leaving the entire city in ruins. Hud and the believers were saved through divine protection.
story_id: Nabi Hud diutus kepada kaum 'Ad, peradaban kuat yang tinggal di bukit pasir Al-Ahqaf (antara Yaman dan Oman). Kaum 'Ad terkenal dengan tubuh raksasa, kekuatan fisik luar biasa, dan istana megah yang mereka bangun. Namun, mereka menjadi sombong, menindas yang lemah, dan menyembah berhala. Hud menyeru mereka untuk menyembah Allah semata dan bersyukur. Mereka menolak dan menuduh Hud sebagai pendusta. Setelah kekeringan panjang tidak melunakkan hati mereka, Allah mengirimkan azab berupa badai angin yang sangat dingin dan kencang (Sarsar) selama tujuh malam delapan hari, menghancurkan seluruh kota dan menyapu bersih kaum 'Ad. Hud dan orang beriman diselamatkan.
miracles_en: Surviving the devastating windstorm that completely destroyed the giant, physically powerful tribe of 'Ad.
miracles_id: Selamat dari badai angin dahsyat yang menghancurkan seluruh kaum raksasa 'Ad yang sangat kuat secara fisik.

summary_en: Salih was sent to the Thamud tribe, who demanded a sign and were given a miraculous she-camel that emerged from a solid rock.
summary_id: Shalih diutus kepada kaum Tsamud, yang menuntut tanda bukti dan diberikan mukjizat unta betina yang keluar dari batu karang.
story_en: Prophet Salih was sent to the Thamud tribe, a wealthy civilization famous for carving magnificent palaces directly into mountain rocks in Al-Hijr (Mada'in Salih). Like the 'Ad before them, they fell into idolatry and arrogance. They demanded a clear miracle to prove Salih's prophethood: they pointed to a massive rock and challenged him to bring out a giant, pregnant she-camel from it. Salih prayed to Allah, and the rock split open, revealing a miraculous she-camel. Salih warned them to share their water supply with the camel on alternating days and never to harm her. Driven by greed and arrogance, a group of disbelievers plotted and killed the camel. Salih warned them they had only three days to live. On the fourth day, a terrifying thunderous blast (Sayhah) accompanied by an earthquake struck, destroying the disbelievers in their homes, while Salih and the believers were saved.
story_id: Nabi Shalih diutus kepada kaum Tsamud, peradaban kaya yang terkenal memahat istana megah langsung di pegunungan batu Al-Hijr. Mereka menyembah berhala dan menuntut mukjizat nyata: mereka menantang Shalih mengeluarkan unta betina hamil yang besar dari dalam batu gunung yang padat. Atas izin Allah, batu tersebut terbelah dan keluarlah unta mukjizat. Shalih memperingatkan mereka untuk berbagi air dengan unta tersebut dan tidak menyakitinya. Namun, karena kesombongan, sekelompok orang kafir menyembelih unta itu. Shalih memperingatkan bahwa azab akan datang dalam tiga hari. Pada hari keempat, suara guntur yang dahsyat (Sayhah) dan gempa bumi membinasakan mereka, sementara Shalih dan orang beriman diselamatkan.
miracles_en: Bringing forth a colossal, living she-camel from the heart of a solid stone mountain, which produced milk sufficient for the entire city.
miracles_id: Mengeluarkan seekor unta betina hidup yang sangat besar dari jantung batu gunung yang padat, yang menghasilkan susu yang cukup untuk seluruh penduduk kota.

summary_en: Ibrahim, the father of prophets and friend of Allah, destroyed the idols of Babylon and was miraculously saved from Nimrod's blazing fire.
summary_id: Ibrahim, bapak para nabi dan kekasih Allah, menghancurkan berhala-berhala Babilonia dan diselamatkan secara mukjizat dari api berkobar Nimrud.
story_en: Prophet Ibrahim, known as Khalilullah (the Friend of Allah) and the Father of the Prophets, was born in Babylon. He rejected the idol worship of his father Azar and the community, seeking the true Creator through observing the stars, moon, and sun. He destroyed the temple's idols, leaving only the largest one to show the people the foolishness of their worship. King Nimrod ordered him thrown into a massive blazing fire, but Allah commanded the fire: 'O fire, be cool and peaceful for Ibrahim.' He walked out completely unharmed. Ibrahim migrated to Syria, Palestine, and Egypt. In his old age, he was blessed with Ismail and Ishaq. Under divine command, he took Ismail and his mother Hajar to the barren valley of Makkah, where the Zamzam spring miraculously appeared. Later, he passed the ultimate test of readiness to sacrifice his beloved son Ismail, which Allah substituted with a ram. Together, Ibrahim and Ismail built the Kaaba, establishing Makkah as the holy center of monotheism.
story_id: Nabi Ibrahim, yang dijuluki Khalilullah (Kekasih Allah) dan Bapak Para Nabi, lahir di Babilonia. Beliau menolak penyembahan berhala oleh ayahnya, Azar, dan kaumnya. Ibrahim menghancurkan berhala-berhala kota dan menyisakan yang terbesar untuk menunjukkan kelemahan patung tersebut. Raja Nimrud memerintahkan agar Ibrahim dilempar ke dalam kobaran api raksasa, tetapi Allah berfirman: 'Wahai api, dinginlah dan selamatkanlah Ibrahim.' Beliau keluar tanpa terluka sedikit pun. Ibrahim berhijrah, dikaruniai putra Ismail dan Ishaq di usia senja. Beliau membawa Ismail dan Hajar ke lembah Makkah yang gersang, tempat memancarnya air Zamzam. Beliau lulus ujian mengorbankan Ismail, yang kemudian diganti Allah dengan domba dari surga. Bersama Ismail, beliau membangun Ka'bah.
miracles_en: The blazing fire of Nimrod becoming cool and safe for him, receiving glad tidings of children in extreme old age, and the miraculous spring of Zamzam appearing for his son.
miracles_id: Api berkobar Raja Nimrud menjadi dingin dan menyelamatkan dirinya, mendapatkan kabar gembira tentang anak di usia yang sangat senja, dan munculnya mata air Zamzam secara mukjizat untuk putranya.

summary_en: Lut was sent to Sodom to warn against their unprecedented immorality, escaping with believers before the city was turned upside down.
summary_id: Lut diutus ke Sodom untuk memperingatkan terhadap kebejatan moral mereka yang belum pernah terjadi sebelumnya, melarikan diri bersama orang beriman sebelum kota itu dibalikkan.
story_en: Prophet Lut, the nephew of Ibrahim, was sent to the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah near the Dead Sea. The inhabitants practiced highway robbery, corruption, and public immorality, and were the first in human history to practice widespread homosexuality. Lut preached continuously for righteousness and clean living, but they rejected his warnings and threatened to expel him. Angels disguised as handsome young men visited Lut as guests. The men of the town surrounded Lut's house, demanding he hand over his guests for abuse. Lut tried to protect them, and the angels revealed their identity, striking the mob blind. They instructed Lut to flee with the believers during the night and not look back. At dawn, Allah rained stones of baked clay upon the cities and turned them upside down, completely destroying the corrupt population. Lut's wife, who had supported the wrongdoers, looked back and was destroyed along with them.
story_id: Nabi Lut, keponakan Nabi Ibrahim, diutus ke kota Sodom dan Gomorrah dekat Laut Mati. Penduduknya melakukan perampokan, korupsi, dan penyimpangan seksual (homoseksual) yang belum pernah ada sebelumnya. Lut menyeru mereka untuk bertobat, namun mereka menolak dan mengancam mengusirnya. Malaikat menyamar sebagai pria tampan mengunjungi Lut. Ketika penduduk mengepung rumah Lut untuk melecehkan tamu tersebut, malaikat membutakan mata massa dan menyuruh Lut pergi di malam hari bersama orang beriman. Di waktu subuh, Allah menghujani kota itu dengan batu belerang yang terbakar dan membalikkan kota tersebut, membinasakan semua orang termasuk istri Lut yang berkhianat.
miracles_en: Divine protection from the aggressive mob when the angels struck them blind, and the absolute destruction of the town while Lut and the believers escaped.
miracles_id: Perlindungan ilahi dari massa yang agresif ketika para malaikat membutakan mata mereka, dan kehancuran total kota Sodom sementara Lut dan orang-orang beriman diselamatkan.

summary_en: Ismail was Ibrahim's eldest son, whose infancy saw the gushing of the Zamzam well and whose submission to sacrifice led to the holiday of Eid al-Adha.
summary_id: Ismail adalah putra sulung Ibrahim, yang pada masa bayinya memancar sumur Zamzam dan kepatuhannya untuk disembelih melahirkan hari raya Idul Adha.
story_en: Prophet Ismail was the firstborn son of Ibrahim, born to Hajar. As an infant, he and his mother were left in the dry, uninhabited valley of Makkah by Ibrahim under divine command. When their water ran out, Hajar ran between the hills of Safa and Marwa seven times searching for help. In response to Ismail's cries and kicking feet, the Angel Jibril struck the ground, causing the Zamzam spring to gush forth. This miracle attracted the tribe of Jurhum, who settled there, establishing Makkah. As a young man, Ismail proved his absolute submission to Allah when he willingly agreed to be sacrificed by his father in accordance with Ibrahim's dream, saying, 'O my father, do as you are commanded; you will find me patient.' Allah accepted their devotion and substituted a ram from heaven. Together, Ibrahim and Ismail rebuilt the Kaaba and established the rites of Hajj. Ismail was an eloquent speaker and the ancestor of the northern Arabs, including Prophet Muhammad.
story_id: Nabi Ismail adalah putra sulung Ibrahim dari istrinya, Hajar. Saat masih bayi, ia dan ibunya ditinggalkan di lembah Makkah yang gersang atas perintah Allah. Ketika perbekalan habis, Hajar berlari antara bukit Safa dan Marwa tujuh kali mencari bantuan. Melalui tendangan kaki bayi Ismail, Allah memancarkan mata air Zamzam yang menyelamatkan mereka dan menarik suku Jurhum menetap di sana. Ismail membuktikan kepatuhan mutlaknya ketika bersedia disembelih oleh ayahnya demi menunaikan perintah mimpi Ibrahim, yang kemudian diganti Allah dengan domba dari surga. Bersama ayahnya, Ismail membangun kembali Ka'bah dan merumuskan ibadah Hajj.
miracles_en: The Zamzam water spring gushing out from under his infant feet, and surviving the sacrifice attempt when a ram was sent from heaven.
miracles_id: Mata air Zamzam yang memancar dari bawah kaki bayinya, dan selamat dari penyembelihan ketika seekor domba dikirim dari surga.

summary_en: Ishaq was Ibrahim's second son, born to Sarah in her old age as a miracle, and the ancestor of the line of Israelite prophets.
summary_id: Ishaq adalah putra kedua Ibrahim, lahir dari Sarah di usia tuanya sebagai mukjizat, dan leluhur dari silsilah para nabi Bani Israel.
story_en: Prophet Ishaq was the second son of Ibrahim, born to Sarah. His birth was a grand miracle, announced by angels to Ibrahim and Sarah when Sarah was barren and both parents were in extreme old age. Sarah laughed in disbelief, but the angels confirmed it was Allah's decree. Ishaq grew up to be a highly righteous, peaceful, and devoted prophet, inheriting the monotheistic legacy in Palestine and Canaan. He guided his community with gentleness and deep spirituality. He married Rebekah and was blessed with twin sons, Esau and Yaqub (Jacob). Through Yaqub, Ishaq became the forefather of the line of Israelite prophets, establishing a lasting legacy of faith and guidance.
story_id: Nabi Ishaq adalah putra kedua Ibrahim dari istrinya, Sarah. Kelahirannya adalah mukjizat besar yang dikabarkan oleh para malaikat ketika Sarah mandul dan keduanya sudah sangat lanjut usia. Sarah tertawa karena heran, namun malaikat menegaskan itu adalah kehendak Allah. Ishaq tumbuh menjadi nabi yang saleh, damai, dan taat, mewarisi ajaran tauhid di wilayah Palestina dan Kanaan. Beliau membimbing kaumnya dengan kelembutan. Ishaq menikah dengan Ribka dan dikaruniai putra kembar, Esau dan Yaqub. Melalui Yaqub, beliau menjadi leluhur para nabi Bani Israel.
miracles_en: Being conceived and born to a barren mother and elderly parents in response to the angels' glad tidings.
miracles_id: Dilahirkan dari seorang ibu yang mandul dan orang tua yang sangat lanjut usia sebagai jawaban atas kabar gembira dari para malaikat.

summary_en: Yaqub, also known as Israel, was a patient prophet who endured decades of grief over his son Yusuf, having his eyesight miraculously restored.
summary_id: Yaqub, juga dikenal sebagai Israel, adalah nabi penyabar yang menanggung kesedihan puluhan tahun atas putranya Yusuf, dan penglihatannya pulih secara mukjizat.
story_en: Prophet Yaqub, also known as Israel (Servant of God), was the son of Ishaq. He was a deeply spiritual prophet who migrated to Haran and raised a family of twelve sons, who became the ancestors of the Twelve Tribes of Israel. Yaqub's deep love for his young son Yusuf created jealousy among the older brothers, who plotted to throw Yusuf into a deep well and lied to their father that a wolf had eaten him. Yaqub was devastated and grieved for decades, crying so much that he lost his eyesight. Despite his intense pain, he maintained 'beautiful patience' (Sabrun Jameel), placing his trust entirely in Allah. Decades later, his faith was rewarded when Yusuf, who had become a powerful minister in Egypt, sent his shirt. When the shirt was placed over Yaqub's face, his eyesight was miraculously restored, and the family reunited in Egypt.
story_id: Nabi Yaqub, juga dikenal sebagai Israel, adalah putra Ishaq. Beliau adalah nabi penyabar yang membesarkan dua belas putra yang kelak menjadi cikal bakal Dua Belas Suku Israel. Kasih sayang Yaqub kepada Yusuf memicu kecemburuan kakak-kakaknya, yang kemudian membuang Yusuf ke dalam sumur dan berbohong bahwa Yusuf dimakan serigala. Yaqub sangat berduka hingga penglihatannya hilang akibat terus menangis. Namun, beliau tetap menjaga 'kesabaran yang indah' (Sabrun Jamil) dan bertawakal. Puluhan tahun kemudian, Yusuf yang telah menjadi menteri di Mesir mengirimkan jubahnya. Saat jubah itu diusapkan ke wajah Yaqub, penglihatannya pulih seketika.
miracles_en: The instantaneous restoration of his blind eyes when the shirt of his long-lost son Yusuf was placed over his face.
miracles_id: Pemulihan penglihatannya secara instan ketika jubah putranya Yusuf yang telah lama hilang diusapkan ke wajahnya.

summary_en: Yusuf was a prophet of extraordinary beauty who survived betrayal, slavery, and prison before becoming Egypt's treasury minister due to his dream interpretation.
summary_id: Yusuf adalah nabi berparas sangat tampan yang selamat dari pengkhianatan, perbudakan, dan penjara sebelum menjadi menteri keuangan Mesir berkat tafsir mimpinya.
story_en: Prophet Yusuf, blessed with unparalleled physical beauty and the ability to interpret dreams, was thrown into a well by his jealous brothers and sold into slavery in Egypt. He served in the house of the chief minister (Al-Aziz), where he resisted the seductions of the minister's wife, choosing prison over dishonor. In prison, he interpreted the dreams of his cellmates. Years later, when the Pharaoh was troubled by a dream of seven fat cows being eaten by seven lean ones, Yusuf was summoned and interpreted it as a prediction of seven years of abundance followed by seven years of severe famine. Impressed, the Pharaoh appointed him as the Minister of Treasury and Food Reserves. Yusuf successfully managed Egypt's resources during the crisis. When his brothers arrived seeking grain, Yusuf forgave them, revealed his identity, and brought his parents and brothers to settle in Egypt.
story_id: Nabi Yusuf, yang dikaruniai ketampanan luar biasa dan mukjizat menafsirkan mimpi, dibuang ke sumur oleh saudara-saudaranya lalu dijual sebagai budak di Mesir. Ia bekerja di rumah Al-Aziz dan menolak godaan istri majikannya, memilih dipenjara demi menjaga kesuciannya. Di penjara, ia menafsirkan mimpi rekan-rekannya. Bertahun-tahun kemudian, ia menafsirkan mimpi Firaun tentang tujuh sapi gemuk yang dimakan tujuh sapi kurus sebagai tanda datangnya kelaparan. Firaun kemudian mengangkat Yusuf menjadi menteri pangan Mesir. Yusuf mengelola krisis pangan dan memaafkan saudara-saudaranya yang datang mencari bantuan.
miracles_en: Blessed with unparalleled physical beauty, the unique ability to accurately interpret dreams, and surviving the deep well and false accusations.
miracles_id: Diberkahi dengan ketampanan fisik yang tiada tara, kemampuan unik untuk menafsirkan mimpi dengan sangat akurat, dan selamat dari sumur yang dalam serta tuduhan palsu.

summary_en: Ayyub is the ultimate symbol of patience, tested with the loss of health, family, and wealth, and healed by a miraculous spring.
summary_id: Ayyub adalah simbol kesabaran sejati, diuji dengan hilangnya kesehatan, keluarga, dan harta, serta disembuhkan oleh mata air mukjizat.
story_en: Prophet Ayyub is the ultimate symbol of patience and gratitude in Islamic tradition. He was a wealthy man blessed with a large, loving family, vast lands, and robust health, which he used to help the poor. To test his faith, Allah allowed Satan to strip away all his wealth, destroy his home, kill his children, and afflict his body with a painful, severe skin disease that caused everyone except his loyal wife Rahmah to abandon him. For 18 years, Ayyub endured this suffering without a single word of complaint, constantly praising Allah. Finally, he made a gentle prayer: 'Indeed, adversity has touched me, and You are the most merciful of the merciful.' Allah accepted his prayer, commanding him to strike the ground with his foot. A cool, miraculous spring gushed forth; washing in it cured his illness and restored his youth. Allah returned his family and doubled his wealth.
story_id: Nabi Ayyub adalah simbol kesabaran dan syukur sejati. Beliau adalah orang kaya raya yang diberkahi keluarga besar, tanah luas, dan kesehatan prima, yang semuanya beliau gunakan untuk menolong sesama. Sebagai ujian keimanan, Allah mengizinkan iblis menghancurkan harta, mematikan anak-anaknya, dan menimpa tubuh Ayyub dengan penyakit kulit parah yang membuat semua orang mengasingkannya kecuali istrinya, Rahmah. Selama 18 tahun, Ayyub menjalani ujian ini tanpa mengeluh. Akhirnya, beliau berdoa dengan lembut. Allah mengabulkannya, menyuruhnya menghentakkan kaki ke tanah; memancarlah mata air mukjizat yang menyembuhkan tubuhnya seketika.
miracles_en: Causing a miraculous healing spring to gush forth from the dry ground by striking it with his foot, curing his incurable illness instantly.
miracles_id: Memancarkan mata air penyembuh dari tanah kering dengan menghentakkan kakinya, menyembuhkan penyakit kulitnya yang parah secara instan.

summary_en: Shu'ayb was the eloquent preacher sent to Madyan, warning them against cheating in trade before they were destroyed by an earthquake.
summary_id: Syu'aib adalah juru dakwah yang fasih yang diutus ke Madyan, memperingatkan mereka tentang kecurangan dagang sebelum akhirnya dihancurkan gempa bumi.
story_en: Prophet Shu'ayb, known as the 'Orator of the Prophets' due to his eloquence, was sent to the people of Madyan (Midian) near the Red Sea. The people of Madyan were wealthy traders who practiced dishonesty in business by using short weights and measures, cheated customers, robbed travelers, and worshipped a sacred tree called Al-Aykah. Shu'ayb warned them of the punishment of Allah and called for fair trade and monotheism. They ridiculed him, calling him a liar and pointing out that he was only protected by his family. When they refused to repent, a terrible earthquake and a scorching heat wave plagued them. They gathered under a cloud for shade, which then rained down fire upon them, wiping them out completely. Shu'ayb and the believers were saved.
story_id: Nabi Syu'aib, yang dikenal sebagai juru bicara para nabi karena kefasihannya, diutus kepada kaum Madyan. Kaum Madyan adalah bangsa pedagang kaya yang gemar mencurangi timbangan dan takaran, merampok musafir, serta menyembah pohon suci Al-Aykah. Syu'aib memperingatkan mereka tentang azab Allah dan menyerukan keadilan dagang. Mereka mengejek dan mengancam merajamnya. Ketika mereka tetap ingkar, Allah mengirimkan gempa bumi dahsyat serta hawa panas menyengat. Mereka berkumpul di bawah awan untuk berteduh, namun awan itu menghujani mereka dengan api hingga musnah. Syu'aib dan orang beriman selamat.
miracles_en: Blessed with exceptional eloquence in preaching, and surviving the devastating earthquake and cloud of fire that wiped out Madyan.
miracles_id: Diberkahi dengan kefasihan luar biasa dalam berdakwah, dan selamat dari gempa bumi dahsyat serta awan api yang membinasakan kaum Madyan.

summary_en: Musa was the great interlocutor of Allah (Kalimullah) who confronted Pharaoh, parted the Red Sea, and received the Torah.
summary_id: Musa adalah nabi agung yang berbicara langsung dengan Allah (Kalimullah) yang menghadapi Firaun, membelah Laut Merah, dan menerima kitab Taurat.
story_en: Prophet Musa, known as Kalimullah (the one who spoke directly to Allah), was born under the tyrannical rule of Pharaoh, who ordered all newborn Israelite boys killed. To save him, his mother placed him in a basket and set it adrift on the Nile. He was found and adopted by Pharaoh's righteous wife, Asiya. As an adult, Musa fled to Madyan after accidentally killing an Egyptian. After ten years, while returning with his family, Allah spoke to him at the burning bush on Mount Sinai, commissioning him and his brother Harun to free the Israelites. Musa confronted Pharaoh, performing grand miracles including turning his staff into a giant serpent and making his hand shine. Pharaoh refused until ten devastating plagues forced him. When cornered by Pharaoh's army at the Red Sea, Musa struck the water with his staff; it parted miraculously, allowing the Israelites to cross while drowning Pharaoh and his army. Musa led his people through the wilderness and received the Torah on Mount Sinai.
story_id: Nabi Musa, yang dijuluki Kalimullah (yang berbicara langsung dengan Allah), lahir di bawah kekuasaan Firaun yang kejam. Ibunya menghanyutkannya di Sungai Nil dalam keranjang, lalu ia ditemukan dan diasuh oleh istri Firaun, Asiya. Setelah dewasa, Musa melarikan diri ke Madyan. Sepuluh tahun kemudian, Allah berbicara kepadanya di Gunung Sinai, mengutusnya bersama Harun untuk membebaskan Bani Israel. Musa menghadapi Firaun dengan mukjizat tongkat menjadi ular dan tangan yang bersinar. Firaun menolak hingga terkena berbagai wabah. Saat terpojok di Laut Merah, Musa memukulkan tongkatnya; laut terbelah menyelamatkan Bani Israel dan menenggelamkan tentara Firaun. Musa kemudian menerima kitab Taurat.
miracles_en: His staff turning into a giant snake, his hand glowing white, parting the Red Sea, causing water to gush from rocks, and speaking directly with Allah.
miracles_id: Tongkatnya berubah menjadi ular raksasa, tangannya bersinar putih, membelah Laut Merah, memancarkan air dari batu dengan pukulan tongkat, dan berbicara langsung dengan Allah.

summary_en: Harun was Musa's older brother, granted prophethood to serve as his eloquent spokesperson and deputy during the Exodus.
summary_id: Harun adalah kakak Musa, dianugerahi kenabian untuk menjadi juru bicara yang fasih dan wakilnya selama perjalanan Eksodus.
story_en: Prophet Harun was the older brother of Musa, gifted with outstanding eloquence, speech, and wisdom. When Musa was commanded to confront Pharaoh, he prayed to Allah to appoint Harun to support him as a spokesperson. Allah granted the request, elevating Harun to prophethood. Harun stood firmly by Musa in Pharaoh's court, using his speaking skills to convey the divine message. When Musa went up Mount Sinai for forty days to receive the Torah, he appointed Harun as his deputy. During this time, a magician named Samiri misled the Israelites into making and worshipping a golden calf. Harun tried desperately to guide them back, but they overpowered and threatened to kill him. Upon Musa's return, he realized Harun's sincere efforts to preserve unity. Harun served as a peaceful, compassionate leader until his passing.
story_id: Nabi Harun adalah kakak Musa yang dikaruniai kefasihan berbicara dan kebijaksanaan. Ketika Musa diutus menghadapi Firaun, ia berdoa agar Harun diangkat menjadi nabi pembantunya. Harun mendampingi Musa berdebat di istana Firaun. Saat Musa pergi ke Gunung Sinai selama 40 hari, Harun ditunjuk memimpin kaumnya. Namun, Samiri menipu Bani Israel untuk menyembah patung anak sapi emas. Harun berusaha keras mencegah mereka tetapi dikalahkan. Sekembalinya Musa, ia menyadari perjuangan tulus Harun dalam menjaga persatuan kaumnya.
miracles_en: Assisting Musa in confronting Pharaoh, and having his own staff miraculously bloom and bear fruit as a sign of his legitimate priesthood.
miracles_id: Membantu Musa menghadapi Firaun, dan tongkatnya sendiri mekar dan berbuah secara mukjizat sebagai tanda legitimasi kepemimpinannya.

summary_en: Dhul-Kifl was a highly patient prophet of Babylon who swore an oath to judge people fairly, fast by day, and pray all night.
summary_id: Dzulkifli adalah nabi yang sangat sabar di Babilonia yang bersumpah untuk mengadili secara adil, berpuasa di siang hari, dan shalat sepanjang malam.
story_en: Prophet Dhul-Kifl (meaning 'possessor of the double portion' or 'one of the covenant') was a highly patient and righteous prophet of the Israelites, believed to have lived in Babylon. He succeeded the prophet Al-Yasa. Under a covenant with the previous leader, Dhul-Kifl swore to lead his people with absolute justice without ever getting angry, fast during the day, and pray all night. Satan disguised himself as a distressed old man and visited him during his afternoon resting hours multiple times, trying to provoke his anger and break his composure. Dhul-Kifl remained perfectly calm and fulfilled his covenant until death. He is praised alongside Ismail and Idris in the Quran for his immense patience and high station.
story_id: Nabi Dzulkifli adalah nabi Bani Israel yang sangat sabar, diperkirakan hidup di Babilonia. Ia menggantikan Nabi Ilyasa. Dzulkifli berjanji untuk memimpin rakyatnya dengan adil tanpa pernah marah, berpuasa di siang hari, dan shalat sepanjang malam. Setan menyamar sebagai kakek tua yang bertamu di saat jam istirahat siangnya untuk memancing amarahnya, namun Dzulkifli tetap tenang dan memenuhi janjinya. Ia dipuji di dalam Al-Quran bersama Ismail dan Idris karena kesabarannya yang luar biasa.
miracles_en: Blessed with superhuman patience and self-control, enabling him to fulfill a strict lifelong covenant of constant fasting, night prayer, and angerless judgment.
miracles_id: Diberkahi dengan kesabaran dan pengendalian diri yang luar biasa, memungkinkannya memenuhi janji ketat seumur hidup untuk selalu berpuasa, shalat malam, dan mengadili tanpa rasa marah.

summary_en: Dawud was a shepherd boy who slew Goliath, became a wise king of Israel, received the Zabur, and could soften iron with his bare hands.
summary_id: Dawud adalah gembala muda yang membunuh Jalut, menjadi raja Israel yang bijaksana, menerima kitab Zabur, dan dapat melunakkan besi dengan tangannya.
story_en: Prophet Dawud was a shepherd boy who rose to prominence when he slew the formidable giant Goliath (Jalut) with a simple slingshot, saving the army of King Saul (Thalut). He was subsequently crowned King of Israel, initiating a golden era of prosperity and justice. Allah revealed to him the Zabur (Psalms), which he recited in an exceptionally beautiful voice that caused the birds and mountains to join in praising Allah. Allah softened iron for Dawud, enabling him to mold it with his bare hands like clay to design advanced, lightweight chainmail armor. Despite his absolute power and wealth, Dawud divided his time equally, ruling with justice, fast by day, and spending half the night in prayer.
story_id: Nabi Dawud adalah gembala muda yang terkenal setelah mengalahkan raksasa Jalut dengan ketapel, menyelamatkan tentara Raja Thalut. Ia kemudian diangkat menjadi Raja Israel, memulai era keemasan. Allah menurunkan kitab Zabur kepadanya. Dawud dikaruniai suara merdu yang membuat gunung-gunung dan burung-burung ikut bertasbih saat ia melantunkan pujian. Allah juga melunakkan besi untuknya, memungkinkannya membuat baju besi rajutan tanpa api. Dawud membagi waktunya untuk keadilan rakyat dan ibadah.
miracles_en: His beautiful voice causing mountains and birds to join in his praises, and the ability to soften solid iron in his hands like clay without heat.
miracles_id: Suaranya yang sangat merdu membuat gunung-gunung dan burung-burung ikut bertasbih bersamanya, dan kemampuan melunakkan besi keras di tangannya seperti lilin tanpa api.

summary_en: Sulaiman was Dawud's son, a king-prophet who commanded jinn, wind, and animals, building a vast kingdom and a glass palace.
summary_id: Sulaiman adalah putra Dawud, raja-nabi yang menguasai jin, angin, dan hewan, membangun kerajaan luas serta istana kaca megah.
story_en: Prophet Sulaiman was the son of Dawud, who inherited both his father's kingdom and prophethood. He prayed to Allah for a kingdom that would never belong to anyone after him, and Allah granted his prayer. He was given authority to command the wind, control the jinn (who built monuments and dove for pearls), and understand the speech of all animals and insects. He famously heard an ant warning its colony to take cover from his passing army. Sulaiman built the magnificent Temple of Jerusalem (Baitul Maqdis) using jinn labor. Upon hearing about the sun-worshipping Kingdom of Sheba, he sent a letter inviting Queen Bilqis to submit to Allah. He had her throne brought to his glass palace instantaneously, which convinced her to accept Islam. He ruled with immense wisdom and power until his passing.
story_id: Nabi Sulaiman adalah putra Dawud yang mewarisi kerajaan dan kenabian. Ia berdoa memohon kerajaan yang tidak tertandingi oleh siapa pun, dan Allah mengabulkannya. Sulaiman diberi kemampuan mengendalikan angin, memerintah pasukan jin, serta memahami bahasa hewan dan serangga. Ia pernah mendengar semut memperingatkan koloninya agar menghindari pasukannya. Sulaiman membangun Baitul Maqdis. Ia mengislamkan Ratu Bilqis dari kerajaan Saba setelah memindahkan singgasananya secara instan ke istana kacanya yang megah.
miracles_en: Understanding the languages of all animals, commanding the wind to carry his throne, and having absolute authority over the jinn.
miracles_id: Memahami bahasa semua hewan, memerintahkan angin untuk membawa singgasananya, dan memiliki kendali penuh atas golongan jin.

summary_en: Ilyas called the people of Baalbek to abandon the idol Ba'l, causing a three-year drought to show the powerlessness of their false god.
summary_id: Ilyas menyeru penduduk Baalbek meninggalkan berhala Ba'l, mendatangkan kekeringan tiga tahun untuk menunjukkan kelemahan tuhan palsu mereka.
story_en: Prophet Ilyas was sent to the Kingdom of Israel in Baalbek (modern Lebanon), where the inhabitants had abandoned the teachings of the Torah to worship a false sun god named Ba'l. Ilyas called them back to monotheism and warned them against their sins, but the king and people rejected and threatened to kill him. Ilyas fled and lived in caves. He prayed for rain to stop, and a severe three-year drought struck the land, causing famine and death. Ilyas challenged the priests of Ba'l to make their idol bring rain, which they could not. Ilyas then prayed to Allah, and rain poured down, proving the power of the true God. Although some repented, most returned to their disbelief, and Ilyas continued his mission with patience.
story_id: Nabi Ilyas diutus kepada kaum Bani Israel di Baalbek yang menyembah berhala bernama Ba'l. Ia mengajak mereka kembali pada tauhid, namun ditolak dan diancam dibunuh. Ilyas bersembunyi di gua dan berdoa agar hujan dihentikan. Kekeringan parah melanda selama tiga tahun. Ilyas menantang pendeta Ba'l membuat berhala mereka mendatangkan hujan dan mereka gagal. Ketika Ilyas berdoa kepada Allah, hujan deras turun seketika membuktikan kekuasaan Allah.
miracles_en: Controlling rainfall through his prayers, causing a three-year drought, and later bringing instant rain to prove the power of Allah.
miracles_id: Mengendalikan turunnya hujan lewat doanya, mendatangkan kekeringan tiga tahun, dan kemudian mendatangkan hujan instan untuk membuktikan kekuasaan Allah.

summary_en: Al-Yasa was the spiritual successor of Ilyas who performed miracles of healing and guided the Israelites with patience.
summary_id: Ilyasa adalah penerus spiritual Ilyas yang melakukan mukjizat penyembuhan dan membimbing Bani Israel dengan sabar.
story_en: Prophet Al-Yasa was the spiritual successor and disciple of Prophet Ilyas. He was healed of a terminal illness as a boy through Ilyas's prayers and accompanied him in his mission. After Ilyas, Al-Yasa acted as the guide of the Israelites in Baalbek and Syria during a time of political instability and corruption. He performed many miracles by Allah's permission, such as multiplying a small jar of oil to help a poor widow pay her debts, purifying a contaminated water source with salt to make it drinkable, and healing leprosy. He guided his people with patience and kept the flame of monotheism alive.
story_id: Nabi Ilyasa adalah murid sekaligus penerus perjuangan Nabi Ilyas. Saat kecil, ia sakit parah lalu sembuh berkat doa Ilyas. Setelah wafatnya Ilyas, Ilyasa membimbing Bani Israel di Baalbek dan Suriah dalam menghadapi gejolak politik. Ilyasa melakukan berbagai mikjizat atas izin Allah, seperti melipatgandakan minyak janda miskin untuk membayar hutang, menawarkan air beracun menjadi tawar dengan garam, dan menyembuhkan penyakit kusta.
miracles_en: Healing the terminally ill and leprous, purifying toxic waters, and multiplying scarce food resources by Allah's permission.
miracles_id: Menyembuhkan orang sakit parah dan kusta, menawarkan air beracun, dan melipatgandakan makanan/minyak yang sedikit dengan izin Allah.

summary_en: Yunus abandoned his stubborn people in frustration, was swallowed by a giant fish, and saved through his famous prayer of repentance.
summary_id: Yunus meninggalkan kaumnya yang keras kepala karena frustrasi, ditelan ikan raksasa, dan selamat berkat doa taubatnya yang terkenal.
story_en: Prophet Yunus was sent to Nineveh (modern Iraq) to guide a community of over 100,000 people. They stubbornly rejected his call. In frustration, Yunus departed without Allah's permission and boarded a passenger ship. A violent storm arose, and the crew cast lots to lighten the ship; Yunus's name was drawn, and he was thrown into the sea. A giant fish swallowed him whole. In the pitch darkness of the whale's belly and the deep ocean, Yunus repented sincerely, reciting: 'La ilaha illa Anta, Subhanaka inni kuntu minaz-zalimin' (There is no deity except You; exalted are You. Indeed, I have been of the wrongdoers). Allah forgave him and caused the fish to spit him safely onto a beach. A gourd plant grew to shelter and heal him. He returned to Nineveh to find that his people had repented and accepted Islam in his absence.
story_id: Nabi Yunus diutus ke Niniwe untuk berdakwah pada 100.000 penduduk yang keras kepala. Karena putus asa, Yunus pergi tanpa izin Allah dan naik kapal. Badai menyerang kapal tersebut, dan demi keselamatan, Yunus dilempar ke laut setelah namanya keluar dalam undian. Ikan paus raksasa menelannya. Di dalam perut ikan di kegelapan laut, Yunus bertobat dengan doa yang terkenal. Allah mengampuninya dan ikan paus memuntahkannya ke pantai. Ia kembali ke Niniwe dan mendapati seluruh kaumnya telah bertobat.
miracles_en: Surviving unharmed inside the stomach of a giant fish in the crushing depths of the ocean, and the rapid growth of a gourd plant to heal him.
miracles_id: Selamat tanpa terluka di dalam perut ikan raksasa di kedalaman laut yang menghancurkan, dan tumbuhnya pohon labu secara cepat untuk menyembuhkannya.

summary_en: Zakariyya was the temple priest and guardian of Maryam who was miraculously granted a son, Yahya, in his old age.
summary_id: Zakariyya adalah pendeta bait suci dan wali Maryam yang secara mukjizat dianugerahi putra, Yahya, di usia senjanya.
story_en: Prophet Zakariyya was a temple priest in Jerusalem and the guardian of Maryam (the Virgin Mary). He was amazed by Maryam's piety and noticed that she received out-of-season fresh fruits in her chamber directly from Allah. Although Zakariyya was over 90 years old and his wife was barren, this sparked his hope, and he made a heartfelt, silent prayer for a righteous heir to continue the lineage of prophethood. The Angel Jibril visited him while he was praying, giving him the glad tidings of a son named Yahya. Zakariyya asked for a sign, and Allah rendered him unable to speak for three days, during which he could only communicate through gestures, praising Allah.
story_id: Nabi Zakariyya adalah pendeta bait suci di Yerusalem dan wali Maryam. Ia kagum dengan kesalehan Maryam yang selalu mendapat buah segar di luar musimnya dari Allah. Walau berusia di atas 90 tahun dan istrinya mandul, hal itu memicu harapannya untuk berdoa memohon keturunan. Malaikat Jibril datang mengabarkan bahwa ia akan mendapat putra bernama Yahya. Zakariyya meminta tanda, lalu ia dibuat tidak bisa berbicara selama tiga hari kecuali menggunakan isyarat.
miracles_en: Conceiving a child in extreme old age with a barren wife, and the temporary loss of speech for three days as a divine sign.
miracles_id: Dikaruniai anak di usia sangat lanjut dengan istri yang mandul, dan hilangnya kemampuan berbicara sementara selama tiga hari sebagai tanda ilahi.

summary_en: Yahya was a devout and ascetic prophet, born as a miracle, who was gifted wisdom as a child and paved the way for Isa.
summary_id: Yahya adalah nabi yang taat dan zuhud, lahir sebagai mukjizat, dianugerahi hikmah sejak kecil dan membuka jalan bagi dakwah Isa.
story_en: Prophet Yahya, the son of Zakariyya, was born as a miracle to elderly, barren parents. From childhood, he was blessed with profound scriptural wisdom, purity, and a gentle heart. He was an ascetic who lived a simple life in the wilderness, wearing camel hair and eating wild plants. Yahya called the Israelites to repent and prepare for the mission of Isa (Jesus), whose prophethood he confirmed. He was a fearless defender of the moral law and was martyred by a corrupt local ruler after opposing his unlawful marriage, remaining a steadfast witness to the truth.
story_id: Nabi Yahya adalah putra Zakariyya yang lahir sebagai mukjizat dari orang tua yang lanjut usia dan mandul. Sejak kecil, ia diberkahi hikmah mendalam tentang kitab suci, kesucian, dan kelembutan hati. Ia hidup zuhud di padang pasir. Yahya menyeru Bani Israel bertobat dan membenarkan kerasulan Isa. Ia wafat sebagai martir setelah dihukum mati oleh raja yang kejam karena menentang pernikahan raja yang melanggar hukum agama.
miracles_en: Being gifted deep scriptural wisdom and spiritual purity in early childhood, and being born to elderly, barren parents.
miracles_id: Dianugerahi hikmah kitab suci yang mendalam dan kesucian spiritual sejak masa kanak-kanak, serta dilahirkan dari orang tua yang mandul dan lanjut usia.

summary_en: Isa was born to the Virgin Maryam, spoke in the cradle, healed the blind and lepers, raised the dead, and was lifted alive to heaven.
summary_id: Isa lahir dari perawan Maryam, berbicara saat bayi di ayunan, menyembuhkan orang buta dan kusta, menghidupkan orang mati, dan diangkat hidup-hidup ke langit.
story_en: Prophet Isa, known as Al-Masih (the Messiah), was born miraculously to the Virgin Maryam without a father, by the command of Allah: 'Be,' and he was. When the townspeople accused Maryam, the infant Isa spoke from the cradle to defend her. He was given the Injil (Gospel) and performed extraordinary miracles by Allah's permission: breathing life into clay birds, curing the blind and lepers, raising the dead, and bringing down a table laden with food from heaven. He called the Israelites to follow the true spirit of the Torah. When the disbelieving leaders plotted to crucify him, Allah saved Isa by raising him alive to heaven, casting his likeness onto another man who was crucified instead. Isa will return before the end of the world to establish peace and justice.
story_id: Nabi Isa, yang dijuluki Al-Masih, lahir secara mukjizat dari perawan Maryam tanpa ayah. Ketika kaumnya menuduh Maryam, bayi Isa berbicara dari ayunan untuk membela ibunya. Ia diberi kitab Injil dan melakukan mukjizat atas izin Allah: meniupkan nyawa pada burung tanah liat, menyembuhkan buta dan kusta, menghidupkan orang mati, serta menurunkan hidangan makanan dari langit. Ketika orang kafir hendak menyalibnya, Allah mengangkatnya hidup-hidup ke langit dan menyerupakan orang lain untuk disalib.
miracles_en: His virgin birth, speaking in the cradle, breathing life into clay birds, healing the blind and leper, raising the dead, and being raised alive to heaven.
miracles_id: Kelahiran tanpa ayah, berbicara saat bayi di ayunan, meniupkan nyawa pada burung tanah liat, menyembuhkan buta dan kusta, menghidupkan orang mati, dan diangkat hidup-hidup ke langit.

summary_en: Muhammad was the final messenger of Islam (Seal of the Prophets) who received the Quran, led the night ascension, and serves as a mercy to all worlds.
summary_id: Muhammad adalah utusan terakhir dalam Islam (Penutup Para Nabi) yang menerima mukjizat Al-Quran, melakukan Isra Mi'raj, dan menjadi rahmat bagi semesta alam.
story_en:
Prophet Muhammad was born in Makkah in 570 CE (the Year of the Elephant) to Aminah and Abdullah, who passed away before his birth. As an infant, he was cared for in the desert by Halimah Sa'diyyah, following Arabian custom. His mother passed away when he was six, and his grandfather Abdul Muttalib took custody of him, followed by his uncle Abu Talib after his grandfather's death. As a youth, Muhammad worked as a shepherd and accompanied his uncle on trade journeys, earning the title 'Al-Amin' (the Trustworthy) and 'As-Sadiq' (the Truthful) for his impeccable character. At 25, he married Khadijah bint Khuwaylid, a noble businesswoman.

At the age of 40, during a retreat in the Cave of Hira, the Angel Jibril (Gabriel) brought the first revelation of the Quran: 'Read! In the name of your Lord who created.' He began preaching monotheism, facing intense persecution, boycotts, and abuse from the ruling Quraysh tribe. Despite the hardships, he protected the weak and preached equality and justice. In the Year of Sorrow, he lost both Abu Talib and Khadijah, but was comforted by the miraculous night journey, Al-Isra' wal-Mi'raj, from Makkah to Jerusalem and into the heavens.

In 622 CE, to escape assassination, he migrated with the Muslims to Yathrib (later renamed Madinah) in an event called the Hijrah, marking the start of the Islamic calendar. In Madinah, he drafted the Constitution of Madinah, uniting the diverse tribes and establishing a state built on religious freedom, justice, and community. He defended the new state in major battles, including Badr, Uhud, and the Trench.

In 628 CE, the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah was signed, leading to a peaceful period. In 630 CE, after the treaty was broken by the Quraysh allies, Muhammad led a bloodless entry into Makkah, offering a general amnesty to his former enemies and cleansing the Kaaba of idols.

In 632 CE, he performed his Farewell Pilgrimage, delivering the historic Farewell Sermon emphasizing human rights, racial equality, and justice. Shortly after returning to Madinah, he fell ill and passed away at the age of 63, leaving behind a united Arabia and the Quran and Sunnah as guidance.
story_id:
Nabi Muhammad lahir di Makkah pada tahun 570 M (Tahun Gajah) dari pasangan Aminah dan Abdullah, yang wafat sebelum kelahirannya. Sebagai bayi, beliau disusui dan diasuh di padang pasir oleh Halimah As-Sa'diyyah. Ibunya wafat ketika beliau berusia enam tahun, dan kakeknya, Abdul Muttalib, mengasuhnya hingga sang kakek wafat dua tahun kemudian, setelah itu asuhan dilanjutkan oleh pamannya, Abu Talib. Semasa muda, beliau bekerja sebagai penggembala domba dan ikut berdagang ke Syam bersama pamannya, mendapatkan julukan 'Al-Amin' (yang tepercaya) dan 'As-Sadiq' (yang jujur) karena akhlaknya yang mulia. Pada usia 25 tahun, beliau menikah dengan Khadijah binti Khuwaylid, seorang pengusaha wanita yang terhormat.

Pada usia 40 tahun, saat bertafakur di Gua Hira, Malaikat Jibril membawa wahyu pertama Al-Quran: 'Bacalah! Dengan menyebut nama Tuhanmu yang menciptakan.' Beliau mulai mendakwahkan tauhid secara sembunyi-sembunyi lalu terang-terangan, menghadapi penolakan keras, boikot, dan siksaan dari kaum Quraisy. Meskipun mengalami penderitaan, beliau tetap melindungi kaum lemah dan mengajarkan persamaan derajat. Di tahun kesedihan (Amul Huzni), beliau kehilangan paman dan istrinya tercinta, namun dihibur oleh mukjizat Isra Mi'raj, perjalanan malam dari Makkah ke Baitul Maqdis lalu naik ke langit tertinggi.

Pada tahun 622 M, untuk menghindari upaya pembunuhan, beliau berhijrah bersama para sahabat ke Yatsrib (yang kemudian berganti nama menjadi Madinah). Peristiwa Hijrah ini menandai awal kalender Islam. Di Madinah, beliau merumuskan Piagam Madinah untuk menyatukan berbagai suku dan kaum Yahudi, membangun negara yang berlandaskan kebebasan beragama, keadilan, dan persaudaraan. Beliau mempertahankan Madinah dalam perang pertahanan seperti Perang Badr, Uhud, dan Khandaq.

Pada tahun 628 M, ditandatanganilah Perjanjian Hudaibiyah yang membawa masa damai. Ketika perjanjian itu dikhianati oleh sekutu Quraisy pada tahun 630 M, beliau memimpin pembebasan kota Makkah (Fathul Makkah) tanpa pertumpahan darah, memberikan pengampunan umum bagi musuh-musuhnya, dan membersihkan Ka'bah dari berhala.

Pada tahun 632 M, beliau melaksanakan Haji Wada' (Haji Perpisahan) dan menyampaikan Khotbah Perpisahan yang bersejarah tentang hak asasi manusia, kesetaraan ras, dan keadilan sosial. Tak lama setelah kembali ke Madinah, beliau jatuh sakit dan wafat pada usia 63 tahun, meninggalkan jazirah Arab yang telah bersatu di bawah naungan Islam serta warisan abadi Al-Quran dan Sunnah.
miracles_en: The Holy Quran (the eternal miracle), the splitting of the moon, Al-Isra' wal-Mi'raj, water flowing from his fingers, and multiplying scarce food to feed thousands.
miracles_id: Al-Quranul Karim (mukjizat abadi), terbelahnya bulan, peristiwa Isra Mi'raj, memancarnya air dari sela-sela jarinya, dan melipatgandakan makanan yang sedikit untuk ribuan orang.
